# Cyrus Hadavi (kuros.io)

Staff software engineer at Meta in New York, working on ads ranking AI.
A decade of ML systems: ranking models serving billions of people at Meta,
LLM fine-tuning at Google, the ML platform at HBO Max, recommenders at
Dollar Shave Club, data science at Hulu and Microsoft. Four years of
engineering management (last two running a 15 engineer ads ranking team
at Meta) before returning to hands-on IC work in May 2026.

Core thesis across all work: ML systems should show their evidence or
abstain. Models that abstain instead of guessing; agents whose authority
is bounded by structure, not politeness.

## Work

- /work/nba-video-analysis/ : Computer vision pipeline turning NBA
  broadcast video into structured game state. YOLO11, ByteTrack, WASB,
  SmolVLM2, OSNet, DuckDB. Detection AP50 0.97, 21/24 players identified,
  physically impossible trajectory steps cut from 47% to under 1%.
  Built solo, offline, on a laptop. Demo videos on the page.
- /work/rakhsh/ : Always-on personal agent on a Mac Mini, taking orders
  over iMessage. Durable SQLite-backed task supervision, semantic approvals
  bound to exact operations, curated memory with trust provenance,
  capability-scoped authority. Private alpha.
- /work/poe-league-tools/ : Desktop assistant for Path of Exile built
  entirely from the game's plain text log file. No memory reads, no
  injection, no simulated input.

## Notes

- /notes/benchmark-the-decision/ : benchmark the decision you actually
  need (SigLIP vs OSNet, retrieval vs veto).
- /notes/anti-correlated-metrics/ : when the metric is anti-correlated
  with reality (zero reprojection error, wrong homography).
- /notes/abstain-dont-guess/ : errors should degrade to abstention,
  not misassignment.
